meaning: Product readiness receipt for the API-first Nipmod surface. It proves hosted API access, public source resolution, read-only hosted MCP, local MCP, GitHub mirror and Gitlawb source sync. It does not claim native third-party platform partnerships.
criteria: public source resolver returns package records
install plans are read-only until a user approves workspace writes
local deep scan is available for files already present on disk
hosted MCP exposes no workspace-write tools
local MCP keeps controlled write tools separate from hosted API use
public discovery documents point to current API and archive surfaces
GitHub and Gitlawb source mirrors are reachable
no third-party native integration is required for API use

platforms.name: Nipmod API
platforms.claim: Hosted package API is live for search, inspect, install plan and archive preparation.
checks: public API routes return typed responses
hosted API performs no workspace writes

platforms.name: Package Sources
platforms.claim: Nipmod resolves public package metadata from npm, PyPI, GitHub, Hugging Face and MCP sources.
checks: inspect and install-plan keep original owner attribution

platforms.name: Nipmod Archive
platforms.claim: Archive prepare, search, confirm dry-run, receipt output and status endpoints are live; durable writes require an authorized Nipmod server writer.
checks: verified archive is empty after seed reset
avoid, high-risk trust and high-risk install commands are rejected before persistence

platforms.name: MCP
platforms.claim: Hosted read-only MCP and local stdio MCP expose the current Nipmod package tools with write boundaries. Local MCP also exposes static deep scan for files already present on disk.
checks: hosted MCP exposes only safe read tools
local MCP install requires explicit write-lockfile approval
local deep scan does not install, execute, fetch, unpack or write

platforms.name: GitHub
platforms.claim: Public open-source mirror and CI surface for Nipmod itself.
checks: origin refs/heads/main can be compared to the released commit

platforms.name: Gitlawb
platforms.claim: Canonical source path and owner claim workflow for Nipmod package publishing.
checks: gitlawb refs/heads/main can be compared to the released commit
claim verification reports missing proofs without treating them as verified

notClaimed: native platform partnership or marketplace approval is required for API use
Nipmod owns or republishes external npm, PyPI, GitHub, Hugging Face or MCP packages
every external source result is a verified Nipmod archive package
public callers can create durable archive records without an authorized Nipmod server writer
wallet, trading, token launch or paid x402 workflows are part of core package use
